changes_summary: Revised plan addresses 18 open flags by: (1) moving `projectPinnedShotGroups` upstream into `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` so `resolvedConfig`/signature stay in sync, plus a commit-boundary projection in `useTimelineCommit.applyEdit` for `'config'`/`'pinnedShotGroups'` types; (2) updating all three clone helpers (`migrate.ts:163`, `timeline-data.ts:91`, `usePinnedShotGroups.ts:31`) to deep-copy `start`+`children`; (3) routing `useExternalDrop` shot-drop creation through `buildGroupFromClips`; (4) preserving Delete-shot's existing delete-then-unpin semantics (rev 1 had this wrong); (5) adding a new Step 11 that promotes clip-level mutations on group members (`useClipEditing` split/delete, `useKeyboardShortcuts` delete/split/Arrow keys, `useTimelineTrackManagement.moveSelectedClipsToTrack`) to group-level operations via a shared `categorizeSelection` helper; (6) explicit `isInteractionActive()` flag on `useDragCoordinator` so `usePinnedGroupSync` can defer mid-drag instead of relying on a non-existent guard; (7) added integration test at the commit boundary; (8) added `useExternalDrop.test.tsx` and `useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` to the validation list. The hybrid model and settled decisions are preserved.
questions: Split-on-group-member: I'm proposing to block it entirely (same rationale as interior resize). Acceptable, or should split deconstruct the group first? Defaulting to block.

ArrowUp/ArrowDown on a group member: I'm rerouting to move the whole group. Acceptable, or should the keyboard treat it as a no-op for group members? Defaulting to whole-group move (matches drag semantics).

success_criteria.criterion: `PinnedShotGroup` type gains `start: number` and `children: { clipId, offset, duration }[]`; existing fields preserved

`projectPinnedShotGroups(config)` is called inside `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` BEFORE `resolvedConfig` is constructed (not inside `assembleTimelineData`), so `resolvedConfig`, `signature`, and rows all see the same projected positions

`useTimelineCommit.applyEdit` runs `projectPinnedShotGroups` on incoming config for type 'config' and type 'pinnedShotGroups' so callers passing whole `resolvedConfig` objects (useClipEditing/useTimelineTrackManagement) cannot bypass projection

All three `PinnedShotGroup` clone helpers (`migrate.ts:clonePinnedShotGroups`, `timeline-data.ts:91-100`, `usePinnedShotGroups.ts:31-39`) deep-copy `start` and `children`; an audit grep finds no remaining ad-hoc clones that drop them
Legacy configs without `start`/`children` are upgraded inside `repairConfig` (idempotent; missing clipIds dropped; start clamped ≥ 0)
`useExternalDrop` shot-drop creation goes through `buildGroupFromClips`; resulting groups have `start` + spatially-ordered `children` from creation time
Drag of any clip in a pinned group is represented as a single `GroupDragEntry`; deferred expansion is removed
Outer-edge group resize goes through `resizeGroupEdge`; per-clip resize handles on group members are not rendered; interior boundary resize is rejected
`useDragCoordinator` exposes `isInteractionActive()` and `usePinnedGroupSync` defers its commit while it returns true
`usePinnedGroupSync` applies updates via `replaceGroupChildren` preserving `group.start`
`positionedShotGroups` renders bounding box from `group.start` + last child's `offset+duration`; the in-array-order contiguity check is deleted

Clip-level mutations on group members are rerouted: `useClipEditing` and `useKeyboardShortcuts` block split on group members; delete becomes delete-shot semantics; ArrowUp/ArrowDown and `moveSelectedClipsToTrack` move the whole group via `moveGroup`
Shot-group menu Delete shot still calls `handleDeleteClips(group.clipIds)` then `unpinGroup(...)` (existing behavior preserved); Unpin/Deconstruct still calls only `unpinGroup`
Property test: random sequences of `moveGroup`/`resizeGroupEdge`/`replaceGroupChildren` always produce contiguous, spatially sorted children after projection

Commit-boundary integration test: a legacy config fed through `buildDataFromCurrentRegistry` ends up with member clip starts equal to `group.start + child.offset` in the resulting `resolvedConfig.clips`, and `signature` is computed from the projected config
`npx tsc --noEmit` passes

All existing `src/tools/video-editor/` tests pass with updates (including `useExternalDrop.test.tsx` and `useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts`); pre-existing TimelineCanvas `data-shot-group-kind="pinned"` failure is unchanged
Group `start` is clamped ≥ 0 in every drag/resize/keyboard path via `moveGroup`/`findBestGroupStart`
Remotion preview renders the same output for an unchanged timeline (flat `clips[]` semantics unchanged)
Now-dead cohesion code removed: `getShotGroupAdjacent`, deferred-expansion fields, collective-extent calc in `resolveGroupOverlaps`, interior auto-shift in `useClipResize` for group members
Diff is concentrated in `src/tools/video-editor/` with no unrelated cleanups
Manual smoke test of all flows in Hard Requirement #2 passes (incl. mid-drag sync deferral and keyboard track-move on group member)
Attempting to break a pinned group via drag/resize/snap/multi-select/cross-track drag/keyboard shortcut/external drop is impossible; only Deconstruct/Delete shot break it

assumptions: Uniform-scale resize policy for group outer edges; interior-boundary resize is removed for group members; split is also blocked for group members.
`useDragCoordinator` already tracks active drag/resize sessions internally (it coordinates them) and adding an `isInteractionActive()` accessor is a 1-3 line change rather than new state machinery.
`TimelineConfig.clips[]` remains the Remotion source of truth; member clip positions are overwritten on every projection so Remotion never sees stale data.
Additive load-time migration in `repairConfig`; no schema version bump.
Selection model stays as-is (single click selects inner clip); cohesion is enforced by the write path, not by selection gating, now that the broader edit surface is rerouted in Step 11.
Delete shot keeps its current delete-then-unpin behavior (rev 1 misread this; rev 2 verified at TimelineEditor.tsx:691-697).
Split on a group member is blocked as out-of-scope behavior; rationale documented in code. If the user wants split-then-keep-cohesion later, that is a follow-up.
No changes to Remotion compositions are needed; verification is read-only.
Pre-existing failing TimelineCanvas test is left alone per the brief.
